title: Rollback must be designed, tested, and time-bounded
description: A migration is only safe if its rollback path is built and exercised before cutover, not improvised when production breaks.
body: Use when planning any migration that touches production data or schema. Every forward step needs a known reverse: a down migration, a feature flag to flip back, or a documented manual recovery, and it must be tested on production-like data before cutover. Do not treat rollback as something to figure out during an incident. Actions: write and run the down path in staging, confirm it restores a working state, and define the point-of-no-return (after which only roll-forward is possible) explicitly. Validation signals include a rehearsed rollback that returns the system to green and a documented recovery time objective. Risk boundary: destructive forward steps (dropping data, irreversible backfills) remove the rollback option; gate them behind explicit human approval and a verified backup, and escalate before crossing the point of no return.
